element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player learns directional control in enclosed military base
Default melee option for early encounters
Basic hitscan enemies establish core combat loop
First significant firearm, introduces spread mechanics
Environmental puzzle solving beyond key collection
Significant damage upgrade, ammo management becomes relevant
Rewards exploration with health and ammo pickups
Visual shift signals environmental complexity increase
High-damage melee threats complement ranged enemies
Spatial awareness becomes critical for survival
Culmination of weaponry and enemy patterns learned
Physics variant challenges player skill in isolation

typical_relief_triggers: Clearing a room of all enemies
Finding ammo cache in secret area
Reaching a safe hub area between episodes
typical_tension_triggers: Opening a door with unknown contents
Approaching visible lava or slime hazards
Entering gothic medieval sections (visual shift signal)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Cyclical intensity building within individual levels, with clear relief points at level exits and episode hub returns. Early levels establish rhythm with moderate threat encounters; mid-episode levels intensify enemy density and weapon variety required; late-episode levels combine multiple threat types in complex spatial scenarios. Episode completion provides major relief reset before next difficulty escalation.

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Visual discontinuities in wall textures and slight misalignments often indicate secret areas; exploration of dead ends and side passages rewards discovery without penalty
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Hidden wall sections (often textured differently or positioned unusually) that open to reveal weapons, ammo, health packs, and skill-building challenges like low-gravity rooms

difficulty_curve.early_game: Episode 1 levels 1-3 establish basic combat loop with manageable enemy encounters. Introduction of single enemy types with clear visual silhouettes. Shotgun and basic navigation mechanics taught implicitly through level design.
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Episode 1 levels 4-7 and Episode 2+ combine multiple enemy types simultaneously. Introduction of advanced weaponry (nailgun, rocket launcher). Gothic architecture increases visual complexity. Spatial reasoning and resource management become critical.
difficulty_curve.late_game: Episodes 3-4 feature dense enemy encounters in elaborate spatial designs. Boss battles require mastery of learned weapon handling and enemy pattern recognition. Low-gravity secret levels provide skill-check alternative progression.
notable_spikes: Transition from futuristic military base (Episode 1, levels 1-4) to gothic medieval environment (Episode 1, levels 5+) introduces visual disorientation and new enemy archetypes simultaneously
Final episode approaches with cumulative enemy variety and complex level architecture requiring synthesis of all learned skills
Boss encounters scale enemy health and damage output significantly beyond standard encounters

notable_level_patterns.description: Levels funnel player through locked doors requiring color-coded keys found in adjacent chambers. Creates natural pacing breaks and forces exploration of alternate routes.
Open central chamber surrounded by alcoves where enemies spawn progressively. Forces player into middle ground between multiple threat vectors.
Tall spaces requiring movement control and platform awareness, sometimes in low-gravity secret variants.
Long passages with lava/slime alongside walkable paths, creating spatial tension without active enemies.
